raw_output:
PR #1 security review, rounds 2/3/4 (Codex CLI 0.145.0, gpt-5.6-sol, read-only sandbox, independent passes).

ROUND 4 (final, head ca27180), verbatim: "findings: []. Overall assessment: No reportable security issues found in committed head ca27180. The merge-base binding is shell-free, unambiguously framed, and fail-closed; no new gate bypass, command injection, credential exposure, or undocumented GitHub Actions weakness was identified. The documented residual risks are not worsened."

ROUND 3 (head 77c4024): Z1 (high) diff hash does not bind the reviewed base commit: if main advances without touching the PR's files, rebasing/merging main produces byte-identical three-dot diff output (identical full-index blob ids), so existing artifacts still pass — contradicting the documented claim that moving the merge-base changes the hash. Attack: split an exploit across two individually-benign PRs (merge a behavior change first; an older attested PR's unchanged patch becomes dangerous on the new base). Suggested binding the merge-base commit id alongside the diff bytes — adopted. Otherwise: "well hardened against the requested Git configuration, attributes, pathspec, symlink, subprocess-injection, credential, and artifact-validation attacks."

ROUND 2 (head 3ab83a2): Z1 (high) PR-controlled .gitmodules submodule.<name>.ignore=all hides gitlink changes from the staleness hash: attest reviews on a benign submodule pointer, then push a pointer-only change to a malicious revision; diff bytes and sha256 unchanged, gate passes — worse than documented residuals because it defeats the code-change binding without artifact fabrication or workflow edits. Reproduced by the orchestrator before acting; fixed with --ignore-submodules=none plus a two-repo regression test. Otherwise: "appropriately scoped permissions, SHA-pinned actions, no persistent checkout credentials, and no exploitable expression or subprocess injection found."

--- Post-review delta note (orchestrator, attested at final head 28f1fdc) ---
After the round-4 legs ran on ca27180, their own validated findings were fixed
and committed as 28f1fdc: the vacuous submodule/non-empty-diff hash tests were
rebuilt around a constant merge-base (mutation-verified: the submodule test
fails when --ignore-submodules=none is removed), diff.indentHeuristic pinned,
merge-base failure diagnostic given an unrelated-histories fallback, docstring
and rule-4 wording softened to name the require-up-to-date ruleset dependency,
doc pin list synced. 44/44 tests pass; git diff --check clean. The attested
hash covers the fixed state. Battery history: round 1 (Claude legs) -> fixes;
delta verification caught a P1 the fixes introduced (orderFile crash) -> fixed;
round 2/3 (codex legs + Claude re-review) found the submodule ignore=all hash
bypass (high, reproduced) and the merge-base binding gap (high/P2, found
independently by both models) -> fixed; round 4 (all four legs) -> no new
validated blocking findings: converged.

findings.summary: PR-controlled .gitmodules ignore=all hides gitlink changes from staleness hash
findings.reason: Reproduced empirically before acting; --ignore-submodules=none forced; two-repo regression test (mutation-verified after the R4 rebuild).

findings.summary: Hash binds diff bytes but not merge-base identity — attestations survive semantic base changes
findings.reason: Hash is now sha256(merge-base-id + NUL + diff bytes); rebase regression test proves identical diff bytes restale. Independently found by the Claude review leg the same round.
